key_insight: Adding site-projected orbital density of states (pDOS) to graph node features injects local electronic-structure information and substantially improves materials property prediction.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical way to boost graph neural network accuracy for screening materials when first-principles electronic information is available.
method: Compute pDOS fingerprints from DFT for each atomic site.
Fuse pDOS with Pettifor elemental embeddings before message passing.
Use a spectral attention-gating mechanism to highlight relevant orbitals and energy windows.
evidence_signal: Compared with an elemental-descriptor baseline, pDOS augmentation reduced prediction error by 22.9% for superconducting Tc and 27.9% for the optical dielectric constant ε∞.
watch_item: The benefit depends on training-set size and can shrink for smaller datasets, so pDOS features may be less effective when data are limited relative to feature dimensionality.
paper_title: Machine Learning Materials Properties by Encoding Orbital-Projected Density of States
